meta.anchoring: gold_label = Tu's own stable label or canonical heading (e.g. 'Theorem 7.7', 'subsection 7.5', 'Definition: quotient topology'). gold_node_id is null this round (a_nodes empty); B/C map label -> node_id once Track A lands. page_pdf is provided as a cross-check for traceability scoring.

D-001.rationale: Exact label match: 'S/~ Hausdorff iff graph R is closed in S×S'.

D-002.rationale: The defining text: U in S/~ is open iff π^{-1}(U) is open in S.
Immediate consequence characterizing continuity on the quotient.

D-003.rationale: Exact label: induced map f-bar continuous iff f∘π is continuous.

D-004.rationale: Exact label: S Hausdorff iff diagonal Δ closed in S×S.

D-005.rationale: Tu labels Taylor's theorem with remainder as Lemma 1.4 (title in parentheses).
Enclosing subsection heading for the named result.

D-006.rationale: Exact label: a permutation is even iff it has an even number of inversions.

D-007.rationale: Defining text: RP^n = (R^{n+1}-{0})/~ with x~y iff y=tx, t≠0.
RP^n as a quotient of a sphere — alternative characterization.

D-008.rationale: Tu's 'Leibniz rule for a vector field' is Proposition 2.6 (parenthetical title).

D-009.rationale: Construction of the quotient topology / projection map — the defining property of a quotient map.
The universal-property characterization of maps out of a quotient.

D-009.gold_label: subsection 7.2 Continuity of a Map on a Quotient
D-009.rationale: Enclosing subsection for the continuity characterization.

D-010.rationale: The subsection that contrasts C-infinity with analytic functions directly.
A C∞ function very flat at 0 — the canonical smooth-but-not-analytic counterexample.
Supporting example in the same subsection.

D-011.rationale: Hausdorff quotient iff graph closed — the section's main Hausdorff condition.
Necessary condition for a Hausdorff quotient (equivalence classes closed).
Diagonal-closed characterization of Hausdorff, used in the proof chain.

D-011.gold_label: subsection 7.4 A Necessary Condition for a Hausdorff Quotient

D-012.rationale: The isomorphism T_p(R^n) ≅ D_p(R^n) — tangent vectors AS derivations.

D-013.rationale: The wedge product is anticommutative — exact statement.
f∧f=0 for odd-degree multicovectors — immediate consequence.

D-013.gold_label: subsection 3.8 Anticommutativity of the Wedge Product
D-013.rationale: Enclosing subsection heading names the concept.

D-014.rationale: Open equivalence relation + countable basis transfers to S/~.
Open equiv relation on second-countable space => S/~ second countable.

D-015.rationale: Section intro prose: gluing square edges -> cylinder/torus (Figure 7.1).
Unit interval with endpoints identified -> S^1, the canonical gluing example.

D-016.rationale: Intro states a quotient of a manifold is often not a manifold and frames the Hausdorff/second-countable conditions.
Necessary condition for a Hausdorff quotient — why manifold-ness can fail.

D-017.rationale: The next labeled environment after Theorem 7.7 (next edge target).

D-018.rationale: Under 7.5: projection to a quotient is in general not open.

D-019.rationale: The proof node attached to Prop 7.3 (proven_by edge); it invokes Prop 7.1.
The theorem-like node the proof attaches to.

D-020.rationale: Under 7.7: the equivalence relation defining RP^n is open.

D-021.rationale: The labeled environment immediately preceding Theorem 2.2 (previous edge).

D-022.gold_label: subsection 3.5 The Symmetrizing and Alternating Operators

D-023.rationale: Built on the open-equivalence / open-projection construction.
Establishes the RP^n relation is open, feeding the chain.
Applies the open-relation second-countability result to RP^n.
Open-relation Hausdorff criterion in the same dependency cluster.

D-024.rationale: Hausdorff-quotient criterion the proof of 7.16 leans on.
RP^n relation is open — precondition for applying open-relation results.
Definition of RP^n the whole argument rests on.

D-025.rationale: 'RP^n is second countable' — its proof is literally 'Apply Corollary 7.10.'
The source node being built upon (anchor).

D-026.rationale: The seed node of the bounded neighborhood.
Adjacent Hausdorff-condition definition the theorem relates to.
